title: Nago — Symbology of Umada
real_world_provenance_note: "Nago" draws on Nagô / Yoruba heritage. Within UMADA it is a fictional, evolving survival-and-care sign system — not a depiction of any real sign language or sacred script. Every rooted term is treated with provenance and care. Fan and collaborator contributions inherit this commitment. This is separate from the in-world Honor & Origin account.
core_thesis: Nago begins before grammar. Need comes first. The earliest known signs are survival signs, exchanged in markets, bread stops, rescue routes, damaged streets, and mixed human/chimera communities. Nago later becomes civic infrastructure, accessibility infrastructure, memory infrastructure, relay infrastructure, public signage, tactile navigation, fan participation language, and the visual identity for Umada. Nago is not generic fantasy writing — it is a system of care, warning, witness, and orientation.

function_families.label: Function families (signage axis)
function_families.purpose: Organizes signage by what a sign is for. Governs signage, wayfinding, and the website's Language & Signage section.

families.name: Memory
families.rename_note: Recorded as a rename, not a deletion. Witness's signs are folded under Memory.

families.name: Environment
families.note: AWAITING design. No canon existed to seed signs from; none invented. Seed with care-of-place / land / water / weather marks when fragments arrive.

motif_families.label: Motif families (narrative axis)
motif_families.purpose: Visual-narrative motifs used for title cards, dossier boards, the Visual Archive, and brand iconography — not public signage categories.

families.name: Wound
families.marks: Broken circle, fracture, scar, split line.

families.name: Relay
families.marks: Double line, nested ring, repeated arc.

families.name: Archive
families.marks: Circle, dot, frame, tablet, witness mark.

families.name: Storm
families.marks: Wave, spiral, pressure contour, concentric disturbance.

families.name: Visitor
families.marks: Threshold, crossing mark, half-mask, obscured dot.

visual_grammar.construction_note: All Nago symbols are built on curves, balance, and human connection. Rooted in the Golden Rectangle and ancient geometry — for legibility and memory, not mystification.
visual_grammar.open_question: Exact vector/CSS-level shape construction against the Golden Rectangle is visible on the plate but not yet formally specified for the site to render precisely.

tactile_system.requirement: Every Nago sign must exist as: gesture, flat visual mark, raised tactile mark, verbal description, screen-reader label, public signage icon, map symbol, episode graphic.
rules: Avoid thin details; use clear edges and high-contrast relief.
Design for low vision and blind users from the beginning.
plate_callouts: Built for all — children, elders, and all abilities.

accessibility_requirements: whether usable by humans, chimeras, cyborgs, or all
in_world_honor_and_origin: Nago was born from the hands of survivors. It was shaped in markets, shelters, and ruins. It was carried by Tallai, Ada, Q, and the people. It belongs to everyone.
promise_of_nago: Nago is care. Nago is us. Nago is Umada.
made_with_what_we_had: Anything — if it could mark, it became Nago.
fanbase_use: Fans may create relay marks. Fan marks are not canon automatically. They are audience signals. They enter the Faraday Box.
tiny_questions: What shape feels most like Bread? — ANSWERED by the plate: arch/dome with three vertical strokes.
Is Bread a hand mark, a bowl mark, or a mouth mark? — ANSWERED by the plate: the gesture is fingers to mouth; the visual mark is an arch/dome (loaf) shape.
Does Danger use triangle, broken circle, or sharp line? — PARTIALLY ANSWERED: the plate's Danger mark reads as a sharp crossed/X form; exact construction not yet vectorized.
Is Witness an eye, a dot, a hand, or a ring? — ANSWERED by the plate's Visual Grammar: Hand + Ring.
What mark would Tallai recognize first? — OPEN.
